key_points:
### Key Compliance Points — Financial Statements: Presentation and Disclosures (Amendment) Directions, 2026
*(RBI/2025-26/188, dated 2026-01-05; effective 2026-04-01)*

- **What it does:** Inserts a new **related-party disclosure** into **Chapter IV (Notes to Accounts)** of the Financial Statements Directions, 2025 — new sub-sub-paragraph **19(9)(vi) "Exposures to Related Parties"** under para 19(9) on Credit concentration risk (RBI/2025-26/188, 2026-01-05).
- **Consequential:** Issued following the Credit Risk Management Amendment, 2026; "related parties" take the meaning defined in the **Credit Risk Management Directions, 2025**.
- **New disclosure table (in ₹ crore, Previous Year / Current Year):**
  - **A. Loans to Related Parties** — value sanctioned during the year; outstanding as on 31 March; outstanding as a proportion of total credit exposure; amounts classified as SMA and as NPA; provisions held.
  - **B. Contracts and Arrangements involving Related Parties** — value awarded during the year; outstanding as on 31 March.
- **Commencement:** **April 1, 2026** (AIFIs may adopt earlier, in entirety).

_Verify with the compliance team before acting._
what_changed:
### What Changed
*(Amendment: RBI/2025-26/188, dated 2026-01-05 · effective 2026-04-01)*

| Provision | Change type | Effect |
|---|---|---|
| **Chapter IV, para 19(9)(vi) "Exposures to Related Parties"** | **Inserted** | New Notes-to-Accounts disclosure table for loans to, and contracts/arrangements with, related parties (definition per Credit Risk Management Directions, 2025). |

- **Effective date:** April 1, 2026 (earlier adoption permitted if adopted in full).

_Verify with the compliance team before acting._
implications:
**Why it matters for NaBFID:** This adds a transparency requirement — NaBFID must now disclose, in its financial statements, the scale and quality of its related-party lending and contracts (including how much is SMA/NPA and provisions held). It ties directly to the related-party framework in the Credit Risk Management Directions.

*Illustrative example:* NaBFID's finance team must set up a data feed that tags related-party exposures so the year-end Notes to Accounts can report sanctioned/outstanding loans, their proportion of total credit exposure, SMA/NPA classification, and provisions — for both the current and previous year.

_Verify with the compliance team before acting._
